setting.note: Adiga splits India in two: the Darkness of the rural Ganges plain, where a servant is born and stays a servant, and the Light of the boomtowns, where money and violence remake a man. The map follows a driver out of a poor Bihar village, past the coal roads of Dhanbad, into the malls and gated colonies of Delhi and Gurgaon, and on to Bangalore, where he becomes his own master.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Aravind Adiga, first published 2008. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The White Tiger, Aravind Adiga (2008). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The ochre and lamp-gold of a country the narrator keeps calling half-lit. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
